title: Confit Tomatoes with Tahini Yoghurt
summary: A really low-effort, high-reward recipe for jammy roast tomatoes. It will yield far more confit tomatoes than you need so you can add them to salads, pasta dishes, sandwiches, or just spoon over toast for one of the most delicious snacks.

method.description: Slice the top off the garlic bulb. Add the tomatoes, garlic bulb and thyme into a tray and drizzle enough olive oil to cover the bottom of the pan.
Sprinkle over some salt and cook for around an hour or until the tomatoes are soft and jammy.
Squeeze the roasted garlic from the tomatoes into the yoghurt along with the tahini and a pinch of salt. Stir to combine and then spread onto a plate, leaving a well in the middle for the tomatoes.
Spoon the confit tomatoes into the centre and sprinkle over some dukkah and flaky sea salt before dipping the bread in. Enjoy!

chefs.name: Healthy by ZOE
chefs.summary: ZOE (@ZOE) is a science and nutrition company leading a movement to transform the health of millions through smarter food choices. ZOE means life in Greek — and ZOE passionately believes everyone can feel better and live longer, healthier lives by rethinking what they eat. ZOE conducts the world’s largest nutrition science study to better understand the relationship between food and health. Based on this research, their personalised nutrition program delivers tailored advice based on metabolic and gut health, turning complex science into clear step-by-step actions to improve your health with every meal. Become a member at zoe.com. All of their recipes are developed by nutritionists to help you embrace food that tastes amazing and makes you feel even better.
